level_signals: 6+ years of experience as a technical program manager in information security
Acts as player-coach to other program managers — mentoring, not managing
Reports into Head of Security Strategy and Governance
Comp range $84K–$126K base (reflects geographic spread across US locations)
Role titled 'Lead Technical Program Manager' in body despite 'Senior TPM' in posting title

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of experience as a technical program manager in information security, with a track record of delivering complex, multi-team initiatives across engineering and security stakeholders; demonstrated expertise scoping, planning, and delivering strategic and tactical security program within a matrixed environment

Manage the Information Security Risk Management lifecycle by partnering with engineering and security experts to implement regulations, test controls, and deploy security solutions across the technology stack.
Working knowledge of security frameworks — NIST, ISO 27001, SOC 2, PCI DSS, CIS Controls — and how they translate into credible delivery plans
Project management and/or security framework certifications (PMP, PRINCE2, ITIL, COBIT, ISO 27001) are expected at this level
Experience building and tracking security KPIs and metrics to measure program success and drive continuous improvement.

Practical experience using AI-enabled or automation-first program tooling (security GRC, TPRM, continuous control monitoring) and a clear point of view on where AI augments versus replaces human judgement in program delivery

A strong communicator and problem-solver who balances persuasion with active listening, possesses exceptional stakeholder management skills to engage with engineering leaders and executives, and utilizes proven project management techniques to drive results.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3+ years of hands-on experience in equivalent security roles, such as Security Delivery Manager, Information Security Officer, or Threat Intelligence Program Manager

Relevant professional certifications such as Certified Information Systems Security Professional, Certified Information Security Manager, Certified in Risk and Information Systems Control, CompTIA Cybersecurity Analyst or Certified Fraud Examiner
Exposure to AI governance, model risk, or responsible-AI program work
Knowledge of privacy legislation and regulations such as HIPAA and GDPR
Experience working with security and risk tooling in cloud infrastructure, hosting, and platform contexts

scope.surface: Security Strategy and Governance (SSG) team within Global Security Services (GSS) — third-party risk, compliance and audit readiness, AI governance, data-driven cyber risk and control frameworks
primary_metrics_inferred: OKR alignment and delivery of GSS strategic initiatives
Monthly KPI and quarterly Board reporting accuracy
Program delivery timeliness and control framework rollout progress
core_responsibilities: Lead program delivery for GSS's most complex initiatives including third-party risk, compliance and audit readiness, AI governance, and data-driven cyber risk and control framework
Set and continuously refine the program rhythm: planning cycles, status reporting, OKR alignment, and decision logs
Apply AI-enabled GSS tooling to reduce manual toil and improve program reporting
Identify automation and AI opportunities in program management and operationalise them
Run risk analysis, contingency planning, and trade-off conversations with senior stakeholders
Maintain authoritative status materials for GSS leadership, monthly KPI updates, and quarterly Board contributions
Act as a player-coach to other program managers across GSS and GTS
Flex into other GSS functions where strategic initiatives need senior program leadership

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Clear team mission and organizational context provided
Explicit mention of AI tooling and automation as part of the role — forward-looking scope
Player-coach mentoring aspect adds influence scope beyond individual delivery
Cross-functional breadth across Engineering, Legal, Product, and Security gives high visibility
red_flags: JD title says 'Senior Technical Program Manager' but body refers to the role as 'Lead Technical Program Manager' — seniority/leveling may be inconsistent or in flux
Comp range ($84K–$126K) is relatively wide and on the lower end for a 6+ year senior security TPM in many US markets; may reflect geographic pay differential rather than a single-tier band
Certifications (PMP, PRINCE2, ITIL, COBIT, ISO 27001) are stated as 'expected at this level' — treated as hard requirement despite no explicit 'required' label

fields_inferred_not_stated: location.type (inferred as hybrid; JD describes US-wide salary band and up to 10% travel but no explicit remote/hybrid policy stated)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred true from 'various US geographic markets' salary language)
seniority.level (inferred from 6+ years requirement, comp band, and player-coach language)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as B2B SaaS ecommerce marketing platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not directly stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
equity_form (inferred from 'equity' listed in compensation package language)
ambiguities: Role title in the posting header is 'Senior Technical Program Manager' but the JD body consistently refers to it as 'Lead Technical Program Manager' — these may represent different levels; treated as Senior/Lead equivalency here.

Certifications (PMP, PRINCE2, ITIL, COBIT, ISO 27001) are described as 'expected at this level' which strongly implies hard requirement, but the language is not the typical 'required' phrasing. Classified as hard severity per rules.

Location type is ambiguous — no explicit remote/hybrid/onsite policy stated; up to 10% travel is mentioned; comp range covers 'various US geographic markets' suggesting US-wide eligibility. Treated as hybrid/remote-us-eligible.
